rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE RAYMOND L. MAR REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order Quashing Deposition Subpoenas And Staying Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Joseph M. Quinn. Petitioner's Motion to Quash is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. The motion failed to include a separate statement in compliance with CRC 3.1345(a)(5). The motion will not be denied on these grounds because Petitioner's non-compliance does not appear to have prejudiced Respondents. Going forward, however, the parties should anticipate that the court will require compliance with the relevant rules and code provisions. Any pending motions that do not comply with the relevant rules and code provisions should be amended; otherwise, they are likely to be denied. The instant motion is denied in part. The test to determine whether the subpoenas duces tecum violate Petitioner's privacy interest is the three-prong test articulated in Hill v. Nat'l Collegiate Athletic Assn. (1994) 7 Cal.4th 1. Under the first element of the test, Petitioner has a privacy interest in his medical records. (See Cal. Const., art. I, § 1.) This is undisputed. Under the second element, Petitioner's expectation of privacy in these medical records is substantially reduced because he put his medical condition at issue in his petition to amend the Trust and he expressly linked his treatment by these providers to his inability-to-work showing. His inability to work is an essential issue in this petition: it's the reason, according to the verified allegations, that he does not have the funds to pay his share of the Trust expenses, which is the reason the Trust must be modified. (See Heller v. Norcal Mut. Ins. Co. (1994) 8 Cal. 4th 30, 43.) In other words, the court will modify the Trust only if it is shown that Petitioner is unable to work and Respondents dispute this claim. According to Petitioners' discovery responses, evidence on this point may well be found in these records. The subpoena is appropriately limited to records that may tend to show that Petitioner is unable to work. Indeed, the subpoena tracks Petitioner's own discovery response. Under the third element, Respondent has demonstrated "need to know" whether Petitioner is unable to work such that the Trust must be modified such that in the context of this case Respondents' interest outweighs Petitioner's privacy interest in these records or Petitioner has relinquished his privacy expectation in the records. Petitioner's motion is granted in part in that the court will order that the records be produced and maintained subject to a protective order to prevent unreasonable violations of Petitioner's privacy. (See Code Civ. Proc., § 1987.1; Moskowitz v. Superior Court (1982) 137 Cal.App.3d 313, 318-319.) The parties are ordered to meet and confer in good faith in advance of the hearing on this motion to try to agree on a proposed protective order. If the parties cannot agree, both sides are directed to submit a proposed protective order. Respondent shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on Tuesday, January 14, 2020.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LYNN F. CHRISTENSEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/11/20. (No earlier date available.) Still no courtesy copies or a proposed order have been received by the assigned Examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B., 14.6 A., and 14.93 G.] The petition was reviewed online as a ONE-TIME courtesy. All courtesy copies and a proposed order must be received by the Examiner by no later than 2/4/20 to avoid another continuance. The petitioner must address the following issues in his verified supplement to his petition: the petition caption does not disclose that full IAEA authority is being requested; item 3g(2) of the petition should not have been checked, as the petition seeks to admit a will; and item 8 of the petition is missing other persons mentioned in the will and does not clearly identify the trustee of the devisee trust and, if the trustee is the proposed executor, who the trust beneficiaries are. Item 8 of the petition must set forth the dates of death of any predeceased heirs if second generation or more distant heirs are listed. [LRSF 14.26 D. and E.] No notice was given to the other parties named in the will. No Declaration of Real Property has been filed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BRIAN EUGENE CAMPBELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/26/20. No courtesy copies. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.93 G.] An amendment was just filed on 1/9/20; notice is required for an amendment. [CRC 7.53(a).] No Declaration of Real Property has been filed. The Proof of Publication does not appear to have the correct, full case number. When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the petition must clearly identify the deceased ancestor through whom the heirs take and must set forth the date of death of that deceased ancestor. [LRSF 14.26 D. and E.]
